name: OmopEncounterVisitOccurrenceView
title: Encounter → visit_occurrence (FHIR-flat, stage 1)
description:
Stage 1 of FHIR→OMOP: one flat row per Encounter, preserving raw FHIR values. Stage 2 SQL resolves visit_concept_id (via class-code lookup), person_id / provider_id / care_site_id (via reference resolution).

Notes on FHIR R4 shapes:
  • Encounter.class is a Coding [1..1] (not a CodeableConcept), so use class.code directly — NOT class.coding.first().code.
  • Encounter.type is CodeableConcept [0..*] — use .first().coding.first().code.
  • Synthea references take the form 'Organization?identifier=…|UUID' (search-by-identifier), so we keep the raw string here; stage 2 strips it with split_part(..., '|', 2).

column.name: id
column.description: FHIR Encounter.id (Synthea UUID). → visit_source_value + surrogate visit_occurrence_id.

column.name: subject_ref
column.description: Reference to the Patient (typically 'urn:uuid:UUID'). Stage 2 takes the trailing UUID segment. → person_id.

column.name: type_code
column.description: First type coding (usually SNOMED procedure code, e.g. 185345009 'Encounter for symptom'). Kept verbatim in *_source_value.

column.name: period_end
column.description: → visit_end_date / visit_end_datetime. Falls back to period_start when NULL.

column.name: service_provider_ref
column.description: Organization reference. Stage 2 resolves to care_site_id via the trailing UUID segment.

column.name: part_of_ref
column.description: Parent Encounter — feeds visit_detail in a fuller pipeline.
